title: Webhook, Audit, and Superadmin CLI

story.i_want_to: manage webhooks, query audit trails, poll the change feed, and perform superadmin operations via the CLI
story.so_that: I can configure event-driven integrations, investigate what happened and when, monitor real-time changes, and administer the platform from the command line

rationale: This story covers the remaining CLI commands that round out the full feature set: webhook management for event-driven orchestration, audit and history queries for debugging and accountability, the change feed for polling-based orchestrators, and superadmin commands for platform administration. Together with the earlier CLI stories, these commands provide complete CLI coverage of every API endpoint in the loopctl server.

acceptance_criteria.description: 'loopctl webhook create --url URL --events EVENT1,EVENT2' calls POST /api/v1/webhooks and displays the created webhook including its signing secret (shown only once)
'loopctl webhook list' calls GET /api/v1/webhooks and displays all webhooks with id, url, events, active status, and consecutive_failures count
'loopctl webhook delete ID' calls DELETE /api/v1/webhooks/:id and confirms deletion
'loopctl webhook test ID' calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/:id/test to send a test event and displays the delivery result
'loopctl history STORY_NUM' resolves the story number and calls GET /api/v1/stories/:id/history, displaying the full audit trail for that story in chronological order
'loopctl audit --project PROJECT --since DATE' calls GET /api/v1/audit with appropriate query parameters and displays audit log entries filtered by project and date range
'loopctl changes --project PROJECT --since TIMESTAMP' calls GET /api/v1/changes with the project_id and since parameters, displaying state changes for polling-based orchestrators
'loopctl admin tenants' calls GET /api/v1/admin/tenants and lists all tenants with stats (requires superadmin API key)
'loopctl admin tenant ID' calls GET /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id and displays detailed tenant information
'loopctl admin suspend TENANT_ID' and 'loopctl admin activate TENANT_ID' call the respective POST endpoints to change tenant status
'loopctl admin stats' calls GET /api/v1/admin/stats and displays system-wide statistics
'loopctl admin impersonate TENANT_ID -- COMMAND' executes COMMAND with the X-Impersonate-Tenant header set to the specified tenant ID, allowing superadmins to run any CLI command as another tenant

test_cases.name: Webhook create sends correct payload with events list
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and user-role api_key

Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/webhooks with {url: 'https://hooks.example.com/loopctl', events: ['story.status_changed', 'story.verified']} and returns 201 with webhook data including signing_secret
expected_results: POST request is sent with url and events array (comma-separated string parsed to array)
Output includes the webhook ID and signing secret
Output includes a warning that the signing secret is only shown once

test_cases.name: Webhook list displays webhooks with status
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and user-role api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/webhooks with 2 webhooks
expected_results: Output contains a table with columns: ID, URL, Events, Active, Failures
Table shows 2 rows with the correct data

test_cases.name: History command displays story audit trail
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock for story number resolution returns story UUID
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/stories/:id/history with 5 audit entries
expected_results: Output lists 5 audit entries in chronological order
Each entry shows timestamp, action, actor, and state change details
JSON format returns array of audit entry objects

test_cases.name: Audit command sends correct query parameters
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and user-role api_key
Req.Test mock expects GET /api/v1/audit?project_id=:id&since=2026-03-01 and returns 200 with audit entries
expected_results: GET request includes project_id and since as query parameters
Output displays the returned audit entries

test_cases.name: Changes command sends ISO8601 timestamp
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and api_key
Req.Test mock expects GET /api/v1/changes?project_id=:id&since=2026-03-25T10:00:00Z and returns 200 with change entries
expected_results: GET request includes the since parameter as an ISO8601 timestamp
Output displays the returned change entries with entity type, action, and timestamp

test_cases.name: Admin tenants lists all tenants
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and superadmin-role api_key
Req.Test mock returns 200 for GET /api/v1/admin/tenants with 3 tenants including stats
expected_results: Output lists 3 tenants with name, slug, status, and stats (project count, story count, agent count)

test_cases.name: Admin impersonate executes command as specified tenant
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and superadmin-role api_key
Req.Test mock expects GET /api/v1/projects with X-Impersonate-Tenant header set to tenant_uuid and returns 200
expected_results: HTTP request includes X-Impersonate-Tenant header with the specified tenant ID
The 'project list' command is executed with the impersonation context
Output shows the projects belonging to the impersonated tenant

test_cases.name: Admin suspend and activate change tenant status
preconditions: Config file exists with valid server and superadmin-role api_key
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id/suspend and returns 200
Req.Test mock expects POST /api/v1/admin/tenants/:id/activate and returns 200
expected_results: First call sends POST to /admin/tenants/:id/suspend and confirms suspension
Second call sends POST to /admin/tenants/:id/activate and confirms activation

technical_notes: The webhook --events flag accepts a comma-separated string that must be parsed into an array before sending to the API

Valid webhook event types should be documented in the help text: story.status_changed, story.verified, story.rejected, story.auto_reset, epic.completed, artifact.reported, agent.registered, project.imported
The admin impersonate command must parse the '--' separator to split the admin arguments from the command to execute as the tenant
Impersonation works by adding the X-Impersonate-Tenant header to the HTTP client, then dispatching the remaining arguments through the normal command router
The changes command --since parameter should accept both ISO8601 full timestamps and date-only strings (auto-appending T00:00:00Z for dates)
The history command reuses story number resolution from US-14.6
Admin commands should check the API key role client-side if possible and warn early if a non-superadmin key is being used
Webhook test should display the delivery result including HTTP status code and any error message from the target
All test modules MUST use `async: true` (e.g., `use Loopctl.DataCase, async: true` or `use LoopctlWeb.ConnCase, async: true`)
All tests using Mox MUST include `setup :verify_on_exit!`
All mocks MUST be defined centrally in `test/support/mocks.ex`, never in individual test files
CLI HTTP client MUST be injectable via behaviour or Req.Test for test isolation. Use Req.Test.expect/3 for HTTP mocking in tests. Never use Application.put_env in test files.
